ruleset.name: KernDXFrameworkCompliance
ruleset.description: KernDX framework compliance rules. Ensures Apex code follows framework conventions: trigger delegation, selector-based queries, DML_Builder, LOG_Builder, UTIL_HttpClient, SCHED_Base, UTIL_Cache, UTIL_SObjectDescribe, UTIL_System, UTIL_AsynchronousJobLauncher, UTIL_Email, UTIL_FeatureFlag, REST_* naming, and modern Assert methods.

rule.name: KernTriggerMustDelegate
rule.message: Trigger must delegate to new TRG_Dispatcher().run(). No other logic allowed in trigger body.
rule.description: Trigger files must contain only `new TRG_Dispatcher().run()` (or `new kern.TRG_Dispatcher().run()`). Any additional logic — SOQL, DML, method calls, variable declarations — violates the framework's trigger delegation pattern.

rule.name: KernNoInlineSOQL
rule.message: Inline SOQL is not allowed. Use a selector (SEL_*) or QRY_Builder.
rule.description: Inline SOQL queries ([SELECT ...]) and Database.query() calls are not allowed. Selectors use the inherited `query` property which returns a QRY_Builder. All other code should call selectors or use QRY_Builder directly. Framework infrastructure that requires raw SOQL uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //SoqlExpression | //MethodCallExpression[ lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.query' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.querywithbinds' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.countquery' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.countquerywithbinds' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.getquerylocator' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.getquerylocatorwithbinds' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.getcursor' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.getcursorwithbinds' ]

rule.name: KernNoDirectDML
rule.message: Direct DML is not allowed. Use DML_Builder for insert, update, delete, upsert, and undelete operations.
rule.description:
Direct DML statements (insert, update, delete, upsert, undelete, merge) and Database.* DML methods (Database.insert, Database.update, etc.) must be replaced with DML_Builder for transactional safety, consistent error handling, and sharing enforcement.
Framework infrastructure uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //*[ self::DmlInsertStatement or self::DmlUpdateStatement or self::DmlDeleteStatement or self::DmlUpsertStatement or self::DmlUndeleteStatement or self::DmlMergeStatement ] | //MethodCallExpression[ lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.insert' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.update' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.delete' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.upsert' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.undelete' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.merge' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.convertlead' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.emptyrecyclebin' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.insertasync' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.updateasync' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.deleteasync' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.insertimmediate' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.updateimmediate' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.deleteimmediate' ]

rule.name: KernNoSystemDebug
rule.message: System.debug() is not allowed. Use LOG_Builder for structured, async logging.
rule.description: System.debug() produces unstructured, non-persistent log output. Use LOG_Builder for structured logging via platform events: LOG_Builder.build().info('message').emitAt('Class.method')

rule.name: KernNoRawHttp
rule.message: Raw HTTP classes are not allowed. Use UTIL_HttpClient for HTTP callouts.
rule.description: Direct instantiation of HttpRequest or Http bypasses framework features: retry logic, circuit breakers, logging, named credential integration, and mock support. Use UTIL_HttpClient static factories: UTIL_HttpClient.get(), .post(), .put(), .del(), .patch()

rule.name: KernUseSchedulerBase
rule.message: Schedulable classes must extend SCHED_Base instead of implementing Schedulable directly.
rule.description:
Classes implementing the Schedulable interface directly bypass the framework's scheduling infrastructure. Extend SCHED_Base instead, which provides parameter resolution, metadata-driven scheduling via ScheduledJob__c, and structured error handling.
Framework infrastructure uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

rule.name: KernNoRawSchedule
rule.message: System.schedule() is not allowed. Use SCHED_Base with ScheduledJob__c for metadata-driven scheduling.
rule.description: Direct System.schedule() calls bypass the framework's scheduling infrastructure. Use SCHED_Base with ScheduledJob__c records for metadata-driven scheduling that supports monitoring, parameter passing, and declarative configuration.

rule.name: KernNoRawEventPublish
rule.message: EventBus.publish() is not allowed. Use LOG_Builder or framework event publishing.
rule.description:
Direct EventBus.publish() calls bypass the framework's event publishing infrastructure. Use LOG_Builder for log entry events or the appropriate framework mechanism for domain events.
Framework infrastructure uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

rule.name: KernNoLegacyAssert
rule.message: Legacy System.assert*() methods are not allowed. Use Assert.isTrue(), Assert.areEqual(), Assert.areNotEqual(), etc.
rule.description: The legacy System.assert(), System.assertEquals(), and System.assertNotEquals() methods should be replaced with the modern Assert class methods (Assert.isTrue, Assert.areEqual, etc.) which provide clearer method names and better failure messages.

rule.name: KernUseTestBuilder
rule.message: Direct SObject construction in tests is not allowed. Use TST_Builder.of(SObjectType).build() for test data.
rule.description:
Test classes should use TST_Builder for record creation instead of direct SObject construction (new Account(...), new Contact(...), etc.). TST_Builder ensures consistent defaults, respects trigger framework configuration, and reduces test maintenance.
This rule only fires inside @IsTest classes. Non-test code is not affected (SObject construction in production code is a different concern handled by DML_Builder rules).

rule.name: KernNoRawCache
rule.message: Direct Cache.Org/Cache.Session access is not allowed. Use UTIL_Cache for platform caching.
rule.description:
Direct access to Cache.Org and Cache.Session bypasses the framework's caching abstraction. Use UTIL_Cache which provides automatic partition management, graceful degradation when cache is unavailable, and consistent key namespacing.
Example: UTIL_Cache.org().get(key) / UTIL_Cache.org().put(key, value)

rule.name: KernNoRawDescribe
rule.message: Schema.getGlobalDescribe() is not allowed. Use UTIL_SObjectDescribe for cached describe calls.
rule.description:
Schema.getGlobalDescribe() is expensive and uncached. Use UTIL_SObjectDescribe which caches describe results and provides type-safe accessors for fields, record types, and object metadata.
Note: Individual .getDescribe() calls on SObjectField and SObjectType tokens are acceptable and not flagged by this rule.

rule.name: KernNoRawTypeForName
rule.message: Type.forName() is not allowed. Use UTIL_System.getTypeForClassName() for subscriber-safe type resolution.
rule.description: Type.forName() does not account for namespace resolution in managed package contexts. Use UTIL_System.getTypeForClassName() which chains through subscriber-first resolution, namespace prefixing, and custom ClassTypeResolver__mdt resolvers.

rule.name: KernNoRawEnqueueJob
rule.message: System.enqueueJob() is not allowed. Use UTIL_AsynchronousJobLauncher for async job execution.
rule.description: Direct System.enqueueJob() calls bypass the framework's async processing infrastructure. Use UTIL_AsynchronousJobLauncher which provides stack depth tracking, governor limit awareness, and fallback strategies when queueable limits are reached.

rule.name: KernNoRawEmail
rule.message: Direct Messaging API usage is not allowed. Use UTIL_Email for sending emails.
rule.description: Direct Messaging API usage (Messaging.sendEmail, new SingleEmailMessage, Messaging.renderStoredEmailTemplate) bypasses the framework's email abstraction. Use UTIL_Email which provides template management, error handling, and logging integration.

rule.name: KernRestResourceNaming
rule.message: @RestResource classes must follow the REST_* naming convention and delegate to API_Dispatcher.
rule.description: Classes annotated with @RestResource must follow the REST_* naming convention. REST_* classes serve as thin routing layers that delegate to API_Dispatcher.processInboundService(), with business logic in API_* classes extending API_Inbound.

rule.name: KernNoRawHttpMock
rule.message: Direct HttpCalloutMock/WebServiceMock implementations are not allowed. Use API_MockFactory for test mocking.
rule.description:
Implementing HttpCalloutMock or WebServiceMock directly bypasses the framework's mock infrastructure. Use API_MockFactory which provides consistent mock setup, automatic response configuration from ApiSetting__mdt, and integration with the API framework's test helpers.
Example: API_MockFactory.forService(serviceName).body(json).register()

rule.name: KernNoRawRestContext
rule.message: Direct RestContext access is not allowed. Use the API_Inbound framework (request/response properties) for inbound REST services.
rule.description:
Direct access to RestContext.request and RestContext.response bypasses the framework's inbound API infrastructure. REST_* classes should only delegate to API_Dispatcher.processInboundService(), and API_Inbound subclasses access request/response via inherited properties that provide automatic logging, error handling, and idempotency support.
Framework infrastructure uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: KernNoRawFeatureManagement
rule.message: FeatureManagement methods are not allowed. Use UTIL_FeatureFlag.isEnabled() for feature flag checks.
rule.description: Direct FeatureManagement API calls bypass the framework's feature flag abstraction. Use UTIL_FeatureFlag.isEnabled() which provides metadata-driven feature flags via FeatureFlag__mdt, caching, and consistent flag naming conventions.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: KernNoRawCrypto
rule.message: Direct Crypto class usage is not allowed. Use UTIL_Crypto for hashing, encryption, and key generation.
rule.description:
Direct Crypto class calls bypass the framework's cryptographic utilities. Use UTIL_Crypto which provides simplified APIs for common operations: generateMD5Hash, generateSHA1Hash, generateSHA256Hash, generateEncryptionKey, encryptWithIntegrity, decryptWithIntegrity.
Framework infrastructure uses @SuppressWarnings to opt out.

rule.name: KernNoCoverageTheatre
rule.message: Test method exhibits a coverage-theatre pattern (no assertions, empty catch, Boolean exceptionThrown flag, or Assert.isNotNull after TST_Builder.build()).

property.name: xpath

rule.name: KernCoverageExemptRequiresReason
rule.message: @SuppressWarnings('PMD.KernNoCoverageTheatre') blocks a priority-1 quality rule. Add a comment above the annotation explaining why the exemption is legitimate, and narrow the suppression to the specific method.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: KernNoInlineDmlInTests
rule.message: Inline DML inside a test class is not allowed. Use TST_Builder.of(SObjectType).build() for record creation, or DML_Builder for explicit DML scenarios.
rule.description:
Test classes should create records via TST_Builder (which integrates with the trigger framework and produces consistent defaults) and perform explicit DML via DML_Builder. Direct insert/update/delete/upsert/undelete/merge statements and Database.* DML calls are flagged.
Allowlisted test classes (framework components whose purpose is exercising DML primitives): - TST_Builder_TEST - DML_Builder_TEST - DML_Transaction_TEST - DML_SharingProxy_TEST
Other test classes that legitimately require direct DML (e.g. User insertion for System.runAs, platform event seeding) should use @SuppressWarnings('PMD.KernNoInlineDmlInTests') with an adjacent comment explaining why.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //*[ self::DmlInsertStatement or self::DmlUpdateStatement or self::DmlDeleteStatement or self::DmlUpsertStatement or self::DmlUndeleteStatement or self::DmlMergeStatement ][ ancestor::UserClass[ ModifierNode/Annotation[lower-case(@Name) = 'istest'] and not(@SimpleName = 'TST_Builder_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_Builder_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_Transaction_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_SharingProxy_TEST') ] ] | //MethodCallExpression[ ( lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.insert' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.update' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.delete' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.upsert' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.undelete' or lower-case(@FullMethodName) = 'database.merge' ) and ancestor::UserClass[ ModifierNode/Annotation[lower-case(@Name) = 'istest'] and not(@SimpleName = 'TST_Builder_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_Builder_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_Transaction_TEST') and not(@SimpleName = 'DML_SharingProxy_TEST') ] ]

rule.name: KernNoBooleanExceptionThrown
rule.message: The 'Boolean exceptionThrown' try/catch pattern is forbidden. Use Assert.fail('Should throw ...') + Assert.isInstanceOfType(error, ExceptionClass.class, ...) instead.

rule.name: KernSecurityBypassCallSite
rule.message: Security bypass call site — confirm this is intentional and reviewed. Acknowledge with @SuppressWarnings('PMD.KernSecurityBypassCallSite') or // NOPMD with a reason.
rule.description: Flags KernDX security-bypass call sites: QRY_Builder/DML_Builder withSystemMode(), bypassSharing(), withoutSecurity(), UTIL_ValidationRule bypassObject()/bypassGroup()/bypassRule(), and TRG_Base bypassAction(). These are legitimate, audited APIs — this rule is an inventory, not a violation. Acknowledge expected call sites with @SuppressWarnings('PMD.KernSecurityBypassCallSite') or an inline // NOPMD comment stating the reason; the suppression comment is the reviewable deploy-time acknowledgment artifact.

property.name: xpath
